forum.title: Sky 2025: Towards parallel growth and 0 Core Expenses by end of year
forum.excerpt: Finally, after so many years of work, we are so close to realizing the original vision of Maker: An autonomous financial system that is able to securely maintain a stablecoin, while also continuously growing and innovating, in accordance with stable established decentralized governance protocols and avoiding cumbersome top down, political governance. In my post last week I gave the first look at how this will be achieved: Sky in 2025 update In this post I will provide a lot more specific information, however keep in mind that there is still going to be improvements and fine-tuning of the plan as it is implemented and the ecosystem learns from executing it in practice, but broadly the principles are stable and the final goal is clear. I am really excited to be able to share the details of the full plan to achieve 4 core objectives: 1: Increase revenue with autonomous processes Spin off innovative projects and explore new opportunities in different Star Agents, leveraging the Sky Agent Framework to let them tap into and accelerate the momentum and network effects of Sky and USDS, while also being free from restraints and have the autonomy to innovate and experiment. In order to grow

forum.title: Capital Transparency: Mid-year 2025 Update
forum.excerpt: Capital Transparency: Mid-year 2025 Update The Ecosystem Team has prepared an overview of operational expenses enabled by the Atlas through June 30th of 2025. Atlas Capital Transparency Since its launch in May 2023, the Atlas has facilitated 124.4M USDS and 916.1M SKY in operational spending to support ecosystem growth and adoption, protocol governance operations, protocol stability and security, and the Launch Project (covered in detail below). Sky Facilitators transferred a total of 51.7M USDS and 124.8M SKY in expenses across 11 Atlas budgets in the first 6 months of 2025. Operations of the current Sky Primitives and development of the Sky Agent Framework cost an average of 7.3M USDS and 20.8M SKY, or $8.6M, a month during this period. July 2024 - December 2024 monthly average: 6.5M USDS & 79.6M SKY The 12% increase in USDS expenses are mainly attributed to the Integration Boost program. The -74% reduction in SKY expenses was expected since Liquidity Bootstrapping expenses in the second half of 2024 were a one-time expenditure. New Atlas Budgets Four new Atlas Budgets are now included in this report: Resiliency Research & Preparedness - Supports legal risk management and legal

forum.title: Capital Transparency: Launch Project Close-out and Sky Simplification Transition
forum.excerpt: Capital Transparency: Launch Project Close-out and Sky Simplification Transition The Ecosystem Team has prepared an overview of operational expenses accrued by the Atlas through August 31st, 2025. This is the 5th Capital Transparency report released by the Ecosystem Team providing a breakdown of total expenses enabled by the Atlas since its introduction . Since our last update on operational costs through June 30th , two new Atlas budgets were approved and are included in this report: the Simplification Buffer Budget and Ecosystem Entity Grants. A new Work Area category is introduced in this report, Ecosystem Simplification, to account for the new activities and deliverables that these budgets enable. This report also marks the conclusion of the Launch Project. The Launch Project section includes the close-out accounting of budget cap utilization and transparency of the wind down transactions for unused onchain funds. Additionally, two new sections are added to this report to provide increased transparency on the Liquidity Bootstrapping Budget and Simplification Buffer Budget.
